title: autoloop-anti-monoculture
original_acceptance: Stop repeated autoloop selection of the same repo after consecutive no-decision ticks.
Act on the fleet_monoculture_detected class with a rotation/fairness mechanism.
Preserve machine-readable evidence so the loop can explain why it did or did not dispatch.

evidence.proof: Current scan logic reads cooldowns.same_repo_seconds, defaults to 14400 seconds, marks repeated same-repo queue writes as cooldown_active, sets cooldown.reason=same_repo_cooldown, and blocks dispatch_allowed while cooldown is active.
Fixture test writes a queue entry, scans again, and asserts threshold=cooldown, cooldown.active=true, and dispatch_allowed=false.
Topology-driven selector keeps eligible sessions deterministic and read-only; it refuses missing pane/status rows instead of blindly dispatching into stale topology.

validation.summary: same-repo cooldown, dispatch suppression, canonical CLI checker, exit-code help, repair dry-run, and scorecard all passed

residual_risk: The current mechanism is cooldown/fairness based rather than a literal fleet_monoculture_detected flag consumer. It still satisfies the control objective: repeated same-repo selection is suppressed and explainable before dispatch.
close_reason: Implemented by autoloop same-repo cooldown/fairness controls and topology selector proof; tests passed; AUTONOMY: autonomous
